title: 10 best herbal creams for emphysema

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal antioxidants like curcumin (found in turmeric) or green tea extract can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which may help slow down the progression of emphysema.

Bronchodilators: Certain herbal extracts like ginseng or ephedra can help relax airway smooth muscle, making it easier to breathe by increasing airflow and relieving symptoms of bronchospasm associated with emphysema.

Inflammation modulators: Herbal anti-inflammatory compounds like boswellia (found in frankincense) or feverfew may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms of emphysema, such as coughing and shortness of breath.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with emphysema because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from Ginkgo biloba leaves has been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress in the lungs. This can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema, such as shortness of breath and fatigue, by increasing oxygen delivery to the body's cells. As a result, patients may experience improved lung function and overall quality of life. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may also help slow disease progression.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative helps to protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the progression of emphysema.

Flavone glycosides (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These phenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and damage to lung tissue associated with emphysema.

Ginkgolides (e.g., ginkgolide A, B): These diterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, thereby slowing the progression of emphysema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are primarily used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve lung function and reduce inflammation associated with emphysema.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are also commonly used as they contain ginkgolides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of emphysema.

Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees are sometimes used in creams due to their rich content of bilobalide, a compound that may help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the lungs, thus alleviating emphysema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when they are fully ripe and dry.
Extract the active compounds from 100g of ginkgo biloba leaves using 200ml of solvent in 30 minutes.
Mix 10g of extracted ginkgo biloba extract with 90g of base cream and 10ml of glycerin in 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60c for 10 minutes to ensure the ingredients are well combined and evenly distributed.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before filling into containers and labeling for storage.

remedy_properties: Cytokine modulation: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation by modulating cytokines, which are proteins that promote inflammation in emphysema, leading to lung damage.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in emphysema-prone lungs.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly curcumin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs by inhibiting enzymes like cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), which contributes to chronic inflammation in emphysema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with emphysema because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream reduces inflammation in the lungs, which is a key contributor to the progression of emphysema. By decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress, curcuma longa cream may help alleviate symptoms such as shortness of breath and coughing, improving lung function and overall quality of life for individuals with emphysema. It may also help slow the progression of the disease.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds, particularly curcumin, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, which are key factors in the progression of emphysema.
Tumerone: A sesquiterpene compound found in Curcuma longa, tumerone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of emphysema.
Geraniol: A monoterpenic alcohol, geraniol has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help improve lung function and reduce symptoms of emphysema.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes, specifically containing curcumin, are used to make creams for emphysema due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Curcuma longa roots are used due to their content of curcumin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects beneficial for emphysema.
Leaves: Curcuma longa leaves are used due to their content of curcumin and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried turmeric root and 20 grams of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Combine the weighed ingredients in a double boiler over low heat and stir occasionally for 10 minutes.
Gradually add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir until melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 45 degrees celsius before whipping it with a hand mixer.
Transfer the whipped cream into a storage container and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, common characteristics of emphysema.
Licoric acid: This flavonoid has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe for individuals with emphysema.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been found to have anti-fibrotic effects, which may help reduce scarring in the lungs caused by emphysema, improving lung function and overall respiratory health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with emphysema because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for patients to breathe. The cream's antioxidant properties also help to protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, glycyrrhizin in the cream has been shown to have anti-spasmodic effects, which can help to relax bronchial muscles and improve airflow. Overall, these properties make herbal Glycyrrhiza glabra creams a potentially beneficial treatment for individuals with emphysema.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins, particularly glycyrrhizin, are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms associated with emphysema.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These phenolic compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect lung tissue and reduce oxidative stress associated with emphysema.

Terpenoids (specifically, glycyrrhetol and 18α-glycyrrhetinic acid): These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and cough associated with emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with emphysema.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with antioxidant properties, which may help in soothing and protecting lung tissues.
Roots is repeated here because the roots are the most commonly used part of the plant, so to continue,  Barks: May contribute to the overall anti-inflammatory and soothing effects of the cream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots on a sunny day with low humidity.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm air circulation oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 200 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered roots with 500 grams of coconut oil in a glass beaker for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes using a double boiler and stir occasionally.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by emphysema.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as coughing and shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: The mucilage in marshmallow root also acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with emphysema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with emphysema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The mucilages present in the herb create a protective barrier on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. This helps to ease breathing and alleviate coughing, common symptoms of emphysema. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Althaea officinalis creams can provide relief and comfort to individuals suffering from emphysema, improving their quality of life. Althea flavonoids (such as kaempferol and quercetin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, thereby alleviating symptoms of emphysema.

Flowers: The flowers of Althaea officinalis contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for emphysema patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried althaea officinalis roots, clean and dry them thoroughly to prevent contamination.
Combine the dried althaea officinalis roots with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the roots' medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discard the solids and reserve the liquid.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil with the althaea officinalis liquid in a double boiler and heat until melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a common complication of emphysema.

Mucus-thinning properties: The cream's mucilage content helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel and reducing congestion and coughing associated with emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with emphysema because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce oxidative stress. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica leaves and flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 week.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers with 1/2 cup of carrier oil in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 150-180 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and mix with 1 tablespoon of beeswax to create a smooth cream texture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried marrubium vulgare flowers and leaves from the plant.
Steep 50g of the dried herb in 500ml of cold-pressed olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then transfer to a saucepan.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil to the saucepan with the infused oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale roots at the peak of freshness for optimal potency.
Grind the harvested roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered root with 50g of shea butter in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Heat the mixture for 15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth and store it in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tanacetum parthenium flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested tanacetum parthenium flowers in a well-ventilated area at 30c for 2 days to reduce moisture content.
Steep 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 40c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal extract.
Mix 5 milliliters of the herbal extract with 95 milliliters of a base cream and 5 grams of beeswax to create a soothing topical cream.

Strain the infusion and combine it with melted coconut oil, beeswax, and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil.

Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with emphysema because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and swelling. The calming properties of chamomile also help to ease coughing and wheezing, allowing for easier breathing and improved lung function. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to protect the lungs from further damage, promoting overall respiratory health and well-being. By addressing the underlying causes of emphysema, chamomile creams offer a natural and effective way to manage symptoms and improve quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant activity: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to respiratory conditions like emphysema.
Sedative and anti-spasmodic effects: Chamomile's sedative compounds can help calm coughs and spasms in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, the roots are effective in reducing inflammation and soothing respiratory issues associated with emphysema.
Flowers: The apigenin-rich flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the airways and ease breathing difficulties caused by emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them completely using a clean dryer or air circulation method.
Combine 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 100 ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and let it infuse for 4 hours under room temperature.
Filter the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 50 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of shea butter, and 20 ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized containers, let it cool and solidify before storing at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with emphysema because it provides a soothing and protective barrier for the lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by chronic bronchitis. The mucilage in slippery elm binds to irritated lung tissue, coating and calming the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and constriction of the airways, allowing for improved airflow and oxygen exchange, leading to a reduction in symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm's mucilage content helps to soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by emphysema.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in slippery elm cream have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and damage to lung tissues, which can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the risk of further lung damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are used to make medicinal creams for emphysema because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated airways.
Barks: Slippery elm barks are used to make medicinal creams due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways associated with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ulmus rubra bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for at least 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered ulmus rubra bark with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl at a ratio of 3:2:1.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 45-60 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for about 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with emphysema because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing bronchial constriction. The cream's antioxidant compounds also help to repair and regenerate damaged lung tissue, improving overall lung function. Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making breathing easier and more efficient. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal dandelion creams can provide relief for emphysema sufferers, helping them breathe easier and manage their symptoms with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to emphysema's progression.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the airways and alveoli (lung sacs), helping to alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as shortness of breath and coughing.
Expectorant properties: Dandelion's saponins and other compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with emphysema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots contain inulin, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it effective in soothing symptoms of emphysema.

Barks: The barks of dandelion plants have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including emphysema, due to their high concentration of saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and dry them in a warm air circulating oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Crush 200g of dried taraxacum officinale leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered taraxacum officinale with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping it into a smooth cream with an electric mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with emphysema because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the lungs. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, work to soothe and calm irritated airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, elderberry extracts have been shown to improve lung function and reduce mucus production, helping to alleviate symptoms of emphysema, including shortness of breath and wheezing.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect lung cells from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to emphysema development.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in elder cream, including elderberry and other botanicals, have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, a hallmark of emphysema.

Mucus-thinning properties: Elder cream's expectorant properties, such as those from eucalyptus and thyme, can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms like coughing and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, making them an effective anti-inflammatory component in creams for emphysema treatment.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain triterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve lung function in people with chronic respiratory diseases like emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to your local regulations.
Combine one part sambucus nigra herb with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 160°f to extract the bioactive compounds into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with emphysema because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the lungs, thereby alleviating symptoms such as chronic coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. The cream's antioxidants also help to protect the lungs from further damage caused by oxidative stress, allowing the body to repair itself more efficiently. Additionally, the creams' soothing properties can help to calm irritated airways, making it easier to breathe and improving overall lung function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of emphysema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams neutralize free radicals that damage lung tissue, preventing further deterioration of lung function.
Mucolytic properties: Pot marigold's mucolytic compounds help break down and thin mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory compounds found in the roots help reduce inflammation in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of emphysema.
Barks: The bark of the pot marigold plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe and protect the airways, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak blooming stage.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven for 4 hours.
Combine 100g of dried calendula flowers with 300ml of coconut oil and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discard solids and store in fridge.
Allow the cream to infuse for at least 24 hours before use and shake well before applying.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with emphysema because of its natural ability to ease breathing difficulties. The menthol present in peppermint has a bronchodilating effect, which relaxes the airways and increases lung capacity. This allows for easier airflow and reduces wheezing and coughing. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for emphysema patients to breathe more comfortably.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Peppermint's menthol content can help loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel, which may provide temporary relief for respiratory issues like coughs and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the airways, potentially easing symptoms of respiratory conditions like bronchitis or asthma.
Bronchodilatory effect: Peppermint's menthol content can also help relax bronchial smooth muscle, increasing airflow to the lungs and making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help to soothe and calm airway inflammation associated with emphysema.
Rhyzomes: Containing volatile oils like menthol and menthone, peppermint rhyzomes have been traditionally used to ease bronchial constriction and promote expectoration in patients with emphysema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Blend 20g of dried peppermint powder with 30ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 70°c over low heat while stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30-60 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for emphysema are topical treatments made from natural herbs and plants that help alleviate symptoms of this chronic respiratory disease. These creams typically contain ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, and eucalyptus, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help reduce congestion and improve breathing. Examples include creams containing Boswellia and Eucalyptus, which can help reduce shortness of breath and improve quality of life for individuals with emphysema, allowing them to breathe more easily and participate in daily activities with greater ease.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to emphysema that are treated with herbal creams are chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), bronchitis, and asthma. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint can help soothe and ease respiratory issues by reducing inflammation and opening up airways. These creams may also contain anti-oxidants to combat free radicals and promote healthy lung function.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with emphysema is a blend of Eucalyptus, Peppermint, and Frankincense. Eucalyptus oil helps to reduce inflammation and open airways, while Peppermint oil provides a calming effect on the lungs. Frankincense oil, rich in anti-inflammatory properties, aids in reducing scarring and promoting healing. When combined and applied topically, these herbal creams can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema, such as shortness of breath and wheezing, by soothing and protecting the lungs.
